title: The 2023 Oscars: Everything Everywhere All the Academy Awards
format: Sean and Amanda tape their reaction minutes after the 95th Academy Awards, working through Everything Everywhere All at Once's sweep of the top categories, a three-hour, 40-minute Jimmy Kimmel telecast light on star power, a Little Mermaid trailer that ran mid-show as unlabeled sponsored content, and a closing case for stripping this year's Best Score winner.

segments.heading: Everything Everywhere All at Once's sweep
segments.note: five above-the-title wins, a first in Oscar history

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: Won Best Picture, Director, Original Screenplay, Actress, Supporting Actor, and Supporting Actress: seven wins in total, the most of any film this year and the first to take five above-the-title categories in 95 ceremonies. A24's second Best Picture winner in the studio's 10 years, from directors on only their second feature.

films.title: The Silence of the Lambs
films.note: The last film before this one to sweep the 'Big Five' categories. But it and Cuckoo's Nest were adapted from acclaimed novels by seasoned filmmakers, unlike Everything Everywhere's wholly original script from two young directors.

films.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
films.note: The other Big Five sweep the hosts use as a comparison point.

films.title: The Whale
films.note: A24's other major win of the night: Brendan Fraser's Best Actor victory, which the hosts call the most emotionally overwhelmed of the evening's speeches.

films.title: CODA
films.note: Cited alongside Nomadland as recent Best Picture winners that already signaled an 'out with the old' shift in the Academy's taste.

films.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
films.note: Invoked as the introduction a lot of casual viewers still have to Michelle Yeoh.

segments.heading: A three-hour, 40-minute show
segments.note: Jimmy Kimmel hosts; the crowd gets a taste of vinegar, then goes drab

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Kimmel opened with a fake parachute entrance riffing on the film, then spent much of the monologue on the no-shows Tom Cruise and James Cameron; the hosts say the show badly missed the movie's actual star power the rest of the night.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: Took a few good-natured hits in the monologue and went home empty in every category, including Production Design and Score.

segments.heading: Ruth Carter's surprise costume win
segments.note: the one award the hosts were authentically surprised by

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: Ruth Carter's Best Costume Design win, which she dedicated to her mother, who had recently died. The hosts picked her as a should-win with no expectation she'd actually take it.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Carter's prior win, four years earlier, for the first film.

segments.heading: Best and worst presenter pairings
segments.note: the red carpet got combative, one duo actually felt like movie stars, and Elizabeth Banks worked the room with a barely-there voice

films.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
films.note: Hugh Grant and Andie MacDowell's reunion as presenters, after Grant's combative Ashley Graham red carpet interview ('My suit... My tailor.') that the hosts call the highlight of the pre-show.

films.title: Creed III
films.note: Michael B. Jordan (who directed it) and Jonathan Majors presented Best Cinematography together. The hosts point to the pairing as proof real movie stars still elevate a category, even a technical one.

films.title: Cocaine Bear
films.note: Elizabeth Banks, presenting Costume Design with a foam bear prop and a nearly-gone voice, used the moment to pitch her own film.

segments.heading: Warner Bros.' 100th-anniversary ad break
segments.note: sponsored content dressed up as tribute

films.title: The Little Mermaid
films.note: Melissa McCarthy and Halle Bailey debuted the live-action remake's trailer mid-ceremony, an ABC/Disney synergy play the hosts find at least transparent about what it is.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: Margot Robbie turned up to co-present the baffling, unexplained Warner Bros. anniversary montage, though Barbie itself only flashed in it for half a second.

segments.heading: The Best Original Song slog
segments.note: six musical numbers, one deserving winner

films.title: RRR
films.note: "Naatu Naatu" was the eventual, deserving winner, though the number that made screening audiences lose their minds didn't fully translate to a live stage performance.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: Contributed a David Byrne song to the category, one of the nominees the hosts single out.

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: Rihanna performed "Lift Me Up" live while pregnant, with Michael B. Jordan shown beaming in the crowd, a moment the hosts call an instant meme.

films.title: Tell It Like a Woman
films.note: The little-seen film behind Sophia Carson's nominated song "Applause," which neither host recognized.

segments.heading: A quiet night for the streamers
segments.note: no Amazon or Apple wins; Netflix takes three, but not Best Picture

films.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
films.note: Won four Oscars (more than The Power of the Dog managed the year before) despite being what the hosts call Netflix's 'third or fourth choice' going into the season. Late in the night it strung together enough technical wins that the hosts briefly got nervous about a Best Picture upset.

films.title: The Elephant Whisperers
films.note: Netflix's Best Documentary Short win.

films.title: Navalny
films.note: Won Best Documentary Feature; accepted with a speech from Alexei Navalny's wife and children while Navalny remained in solitary confinement.

films.title: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
films.note: Won Best Animated Feature, another Netflix title; del Toro used his speech to argue for animation as a serious art form.

films.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
films.note: The animated-feature also-ran the hosts say they wish had won instead.

films.title: Women Talking
films.note: Sarah Polley's Best Adapted Screenplay win, the hosts' predicted pick and, they argue, the only award of the night that wasn't part of some bigger storyline.

segments.heading: Shut out: Elvis and The Banshees of Inisherin
segments.note: the year's two biggest starry contenders go home empty

films.title: Elvis
films.note: Austin Butler's showcase lost Best Actor to Brendan Fraser, a result the hosts say they never actually expected to flip.

films.title: The Banshees of Inisherin
films.note: Went 0-for-9, including a Kerry Condon Best Supporting Actress bid that late BAFTA momentum couldn't save from Jamie Lee Curtis.

segments.heading: Tom Cruise's no-show
segments.note: Top Gun: Maverick wins Best Sound, its star stays home

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Won Best Sound, its only trophy of the night. Cruise skipped the ceremony after missing Best Picture and Best Actor nominations, despite having worked the rest of the season's circuit.

films.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
films.note: Cited as part of 'a strange year' for movie stars: a hit full of famous faces buried in blue motion capture, with its visual-effects team absent from the stage for its win.

segments.heading: If we could steal one Oscar

films.title: Tár
films.note: Amanda's pick: strip Best Director from the Daniels and give it to Todd Field.

films.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
films.note: Sean's pick: take Volker Bertelmann's Best Score win away.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: And give the Best Score Oscar to Justin Hurwitz instead.

films: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest

All Quiet on the Western Front.title: All Quiet on the Western Front

Marcel the Shell with Shoes On.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On

The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
